title: Set a fit parameter at datacard level

posts.name: Alexandra Carvalho Antunes De Oliveira
posts.text:
Dear experts
For B2G-23-005 we are combining several datacards for several analyses trying to use a fitting procedure as agnostic as possible.
We have hundreds of datacards per analysis and a couple of different parameters per analysis to consider, they are always frozen for a given datacard.txt.
Is it possible to instead of setting a given parameter in the command line for limit computation as eg --freezeParameters MH,MX,MY --setParameters MX={mx},MY={my}` to add a couple of lines in the datacard.txt that would set its value and freeze it?
Searching in the documentation I could arrive to the following attempt
==================================
MY                          param    ${my}    ${my}
nuisance edit freeze MY
===================================

This does not work…
thank you
Xanda
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts

For B2G-23-005 we are combining several datacards for several analyses trying to use a fitting procedure as agnostic as possible.

We have hundreds of datacards per analysis and a couple of different parameters per analysis to consider, they are always frozen for a given datacard.txt.

Is it possible to instead of setting a given parameter in the command line for limit computation as eg --freezeParameters MH,MX,MY --setParameters MX={mx},MY={my}` to add a couple of lines in the datacard.txt that would set its value and freeze it?

Searching in the documentation I could arrive to the following attempt

==================================
MY                          param    ${my}    ${my}
nuisance edit freeze MY
===================================

This does not work…

thank you
Xanda

posts.name: Kyle Cormier
posts.text:
Dear Xanda,
Could you give some more details on what isn’t working? What you’re trying to run, what kind of failure you’re seeing. Maybe a minimal working example of the bug?
If I try to use this directive on a simple datacard from one of the tutorials with a recent combine version, it seems to work fine for me.
Cheers,
Kyle
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Xanda,

Could you give some more details on what isn’t working? What you’re trying to run, what kind of failure you’re seeing. Maybe a minimal working example of the bug?

If I try to use this directive on a simple datacard from one of the tutorials with a recent combine version, it seems to work fine for me.

Cheers,
Kyle

posts.name: Alexandra Carvalho Antunes De Oliveira
posts.text:
Hi Kyle
“Not working” means “not making any effect”
With a Datacard example, in this analysis if you see the README one needs to set at the command line the value of the parameter MY [1].
This is the only one among 8 (and counting) input analyses in which this is necessary, so I wanted to hardcode in the txt
To make it easier to test on an example the input and modified datacards for B2G-23-005  are visible by the CMS-physics list as guests [2]
That is not a critical feature to ask you to spend time on testing, we can always bookkeep parameters in an increasing command line. I just wanted to know if this is possible using combine, and if it is we can search harder in documentation.
thank you
Xanda
[1] https://gitlab.cern.ch/cms-analysis/hig/HIG-22-012/datacards/-/tree/master/Resonant#resonant == the setting of MY only makes a difference in the “Y->…” cases
[2] /eos/home-e/ejourdhu/HH_fullRun2_resonant_fits/datacards_run2_resonant_hh_hy/ggtt/hig-22-012/Resonant/NMSSM_Y_gg_High_Mass
